rulings.Case Title: NAVY FEDERAL CREDIT UNION VS. FRANK MABRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion: Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order Denying Request For Court Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF NAVY FEDERAL CREDIT UNION's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order Denying Request For Court Judgment. Continued for Reconsideration to November 5, 2025 in Dept 505. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS VS. GAMESTOP CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS' Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff Center for Advanced Public Awareness's unopposed Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts, including the additional settlement payments in lieu of civil penalties (see 11 C.C.R. 3204) are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and, (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: AHLIVIA FULLER VS. UNITED SF PARKING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Discovery Respones, Further Responses, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 11. PLAINTIFF AHLIVIA FULLER's Motion To Compel Verified Discovery Respones, Further Responses, And Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses is denied. First, plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1345 and provide the mandatory separate statement. Second, the motion appears moot because defendant did ultimately provide verified substantive responses. Defendant did not waive its objections because its July 22, 2025, response, which was composed of objections, was timely based on plaintiff's e-service of the discovery. In any event, the parties agreed to a time extension. Third, plaintiff failed to meet and confer in good faith prior to filing this motion. Defendant's August 1, 2025, supplemental discovery responses were substantive, albeit they lacked a verification. Plaintiff should have inquired about the lack of verification prior to filing this motion. Lastly, the parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the production of documents and ensure that plaintiff can access the OneDrive. Sanctions denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: ITRIA VENTURES LLC VS. SC CELLARS LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 12. PLAINTIFF ITRIA VENTURES LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff Itria Ventures LLC's unopposed Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Plaintiff carried its initial burden to establish each element of its causes of action for breach of contract and breach of guarantee entitling it to judgment on its third and fourth causes of action respectively. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(p)(1); UMF ## 1-11.) The record does not support the viability of any defense to either claim. The court summarily adjudicates Plaintiff's third and fourth causes of action in favor of Plaintiffs and against Defendants. Notably, no defendant submitted any evidence or argument against Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication of the third and fourth causes of action. (See California Rules of Court, rule 8.54(c) [party's failure to oppose a motion may be considered consent to the granting of the motion]. ) The third cause of action (breach of contract) is summarily adjudicated in Itria's favor against Merchant Defendant in the amount of $178,548.16. The fourth cause of action (breach of guarantee) is summarily adjudicated in Itria's favor against the Guarantors in the amount of $178,548.16. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to San Mateo County** MARIA ISABEL FLORES DE ARENAZA VS. MERCEDES BENZ SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 13. DEFENDANT EUROMOTORS, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS MERCEDES BENZ SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO Motion For Change Of Venue. Defendant Euromotors, Inc.'s unopposed motion to change venue is GRANTED. Defendant resides in San Mateo County, and it appears San Mateo County is a proper venue for this matter and may be the only proper venue absent any information on where the personal injury or property loss occurred. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 395 & 395.5.) This action is ordered transferred to San Mateo County Superior Court. Defendant Euromotors, Inc., who is responsible for the lion's share of the filings on the register of actions, to pay the costs of transfer. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Omnibus Motion For Procedural Consolidation, Expedited Proceedings, And Order Compelling Completion Of Disability Benefits Forms; Request For Summary Judgment Schedule
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 16. 1 - PLAINTIFF THOMAS GODDARD's Motion For Procedural Consolidation, Expedited Proceedings, And Order Compelling Completion Of Disability Benefits Forms; Request For Summary Judgment Schedule. Plaintiff Thomas Goddard's omnibus motion is DENIED. The motion improperly combines several unrelated and duplicative requests for relief in a single motion. The request to file an amended complaint is improper since plaintiff already scheduled a motion seeking that relief, which is set for September 30, 2025. The request to compel defendants to execute an insurance claims form appears to be an unsupported request for a mandatory preliminary injunction. (See People v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 266, 283 ["In general, when considering a request for a preliminary injunction, the trial court weighs two interrelated factors. The first is the likelihood the party seeking relief will prevail on the merits, and the second is the relative interim harm to the parties if the preliminary injunction is granted or denied."].) Plaintiff has not made the necessary showing. Plaintiff's discovery requests are the subject of another discovery motion that will be heard on October 8, 2025, if plaintiff complies with SF Local Rule 2.7B and provides courtesy copies. At this point of the litigation, there is no basis for the court to issue any advisory discovery rulings. Lastly, it is premature for the court to issue a ruling regarding an unfiled motion for summary judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, September 18, 2025, Line 17. DEFENDANT YUEZI, XU's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Yuezi Xu's unopposed Motion to Quash or Stay or Dismiss is GRANTED. Based on its review of the record, the court finds the weight of the evidence shows Plaintiff did not personally serve the complaint on Defendant Yuezi Xu. (See Hochstein Dec., par. 4; Code of Civil Procedure section 415.10.) Nor did Plaintiff accomplish substitute service (See Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20.) Absent consent, service on counsel is not personal service on the client. The court currently lacks personal jurisdiction over Defendant. The motion is granted on the independent grounds that the court lacks personal jurisdiction over Defendant because the record is devoid of any evidence that Plaintiff properly served a summons on Defendant. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 413.10 et seq.) Indeed, judging from the register of actions, a summons has not even issued. Service on Defendant Yuezi Xu and Plaintiff's Proof of Service-Civil, filed June 24, 2025, is of no effect. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
